title: Level 45
wikitext:
{{Background|file=DeepdarkwoodsofLevel45.jpg}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 45: "Otherworldly Timberland"}}
''
'''The Extraterrestrial Forest'''''


{{Class Variable}} '''Level 45''' is the 46th level of the Backrooms. ==Description== ===The Outdoors=== {{Blurimage|file=1b76bf959aa077d46752ea87cb562906.jpg|caption=An image of the forest.}} '''Level 45''''s outdoors consist of a never-ending, uniquely fashioned forest with convenient terrain attributed to tall trees, bodies of water, and diverse regions. The ambiance is coated in a blue mist, beyond bounds, and a small, quiet breeze can be felt breathing over the entire expanse. '''Level 45''' barely provides an active weather cycle for the most part, nor does it have a rhythm for daylight, as the atmosphere remains in a period of darkness and the surroundings are topped by a timeless hazy fog; conditions stay naturally at a habitable and balanced degree, suitable for life forms to inhabit, disregarding the treacherous factors '''Level 45''' acquires. Random doors can be found in cold areas. The landscape in '''Level 45''' has some unstable elements that are troubling to consider adjusting; suddenly, trees can topple if one is not alert enough. Trees also carry an abnormal height despite their puny width, and a good theory, perhaps, as to why the trees fall. The wood from these trees is a special sort of wood compared to typical wood across any level in the Backrooms, ignoring anomalous and secret levels. The tree materials comprise a softwood similar to pine and vary in tints of grey and a pale, dingy blue color, and also include an abundance of blunt splinters, which may provoke pain during physical contact with an individual touching it. There is an anomaly that exists only within '''Level 45''' and other similar-looking levels — mainly forest levels — in which the concentration of the trees simultaneously increases. This designates that the trees close in on themselves as if they are sentient entities capable of motion. The circumstances of this event remain undetermined; the probability of its materialization increases as one advances deeper into the level. The distance, like its prospect, is unknown; it is estimated that the length one must traverse before succumbing to this anomaly is 100 to 500 miles. Death due to suffocation caused by this anomaly is common; it is highly advised not to proceed to venture deep into the level unless there is a good reason to. {{Blurimage|file=25c8c16ba8e9f72d32bfbd6c3c5e4fcd.jpg|caption=An image of a river.}} There is an exclusive edible plant at this level called Neonberries. As the name suggests, these are conventional and standard berries many people can remember, but are subsumed by the self-explanatory gas called neon and therefore emit a bright glow regardless of the fleshy structure. Neonberries' traits are similar to Neon Water's as well; individual colors can produce a specific energy when the food is consumed, where red neonberries are responsible for increasing blood flow, turquoise ones induce a sensation of relief and relaxation, and yellow can either increase the sharpness of anyone's vision while wearing off for a while or make the person that ingested the food's brain doze off and have tough trouble remembering for a median period of time. The colors for Neonberries that should be avoided are purple and green, whereas purple's conditions can inflict psychological torment for a long duration, and green will impose threatening and physical symptoms (strep throat, vomiting, asthma, diarrhea, inflammation) that eventually wear off but for a long and egregious span.
As for their contingent regarding a special fluid substance, Neon Water provides a luminous shine and a reassuring view to the eye. Neon Water is an unusual, safe-to-consume liquid that can also preserve a warming passion and ease muscles that have been tense in advance. This element is peculiar in that it enhances sensitivity when ingested, responsible for senses such as smell, perception, visual acuity, and mentality. Nevertheless, death due to an overdose of Neon Water is quite common. Neon Water particularly contains an unknown chemical which, if consumed in high doses, will cause severe paralysis and poisoning (3 liters is the best unit for drinking Neon Water daily). However, touching or submerging oneself in physical contact with it does not cause injury. ===The Underground=== {{Blurimage|file=48693429148 ce5702cffb k.jpg|caption=An entrance of a cave from '''Level 45''' flowing with Neon Water in a pocket-space.}} Level 45's underground area is comprised of a single expansive cave system. It is not dictated in any particular formation, its geometry is thoroughly different from the Frontrooms, it is simply impossible for the dissolution of limestone to form such cave; unnaturally smooth texture of some regions and structure-like; formations inordinately supported by thin substructures without collapsing; an expansive region possessing the surface to that of a small planet; and peculiarly deep pitfalls and tunnels either going further into the underground or are entrances to the cave itself. Many sufficient plants occupy the upper layers of caves, providing unique and incomprehensible life forms; the most notable feature of this relationship is the common sightings of bright blue neon mushrooms that have managed to thrive and flourish throughout the higher heights of the caves. It's innermost for discerning discrepancies and differences from the approval of the bright lights scattered roughly across the open giant spaces within potholes. The support of the luminous and minuscule pockets of light radiates from the translucent organisms,'''Glow Larvae'''. One can describe the caves upon entering as cold, moist, icy, and humid. {{Blurimage|file=Neon crystal Level 45.jpg|caption=A carved sample of a '''Pictonite'''.}} A brand crystal implanted within the subterranean of '''Level 45''' is worth over $ 50,000 (USD - United States Dollars) compared to the year 2020's global economy. This crystal is called Pictonite; the name is derived from the hexadecimal color "Picton," corresponding to a light blue, equivalent to the bright pigment of neon gemstones. The density of this diaphanous stone is eminently inconsequential and is mainly compressed with air for the neon gas it contains to travel freely; without this certain element, the evident ornament itself wouldn't be a hefty value for discussions and marketing purposes. The nobility of '''Pictonite''' is an extravagant evaluation originating with the inferior depths it is most universal in; the deeper and volatile environments often populated by hazardous entities are a more commonplace for contributing to this jewel, making it highly advised to equip protection before searching for these minerals, since in-fact they have nearly the greatest odds of growing in natural spaces rather than physically mining for an outcome. ==Additional Information==
Recovered Note:
''Predetermination''

Miraculously, I have seemed to outrun the wretched beast that inhabits these inscrutable caves. I am currently writing this note inside of a tiny cavity with no escape routes or hiding spots to protect me, as the creature is perhaps searching for me at this rate. I hope I get to see the dark night and blue sky once again, and I hope that I have the resolve to keep this up and linger my patience long enough. This creature. I do not have enough knowledge of it, and how foolish for me to step into the territory without the wisdom of its presence. It is something tall, with human-like proportions. The empty flesh a brain cannot comprehend and would never want to experience the horrific visual exposure from it ever again. The dissatisfying and selfish aroma emanating out of it is an unforgivable and never-before scent, ever sophisticated and imagined beyond the capability of the brain to fathom. I refuse to call this thing a humanoid; it is not a mortal and will never regain good morals. It knows we're clear of its intentions, and it will do whatever it takes to persevere and achieve its mission. {{Blurimage|file=Level45cavereflection.jpg|caption=It's right there.}} My ignorance was my downfall, for I hadn't considered seeking deeper information about this being. I should have been cautious. I should have come in prepared against the odds of danger. I underestimated the proximity and what boundaries I had to respect. I am neglectful. Once an optimist, I was now a pessimist. I contemplate whether it was my fault; was it me who caused this down-spiral? I am not certain. Time is short, and I may not be able to make it. I am waiting for death to approach, but it is impending super slowly. I am doomed. It is '''my''' impending doom. By the time I least expect it, I am sure my face will be pried out of me and my muscles exposed to the burning reaction. God is making me sulk in for my consequences before I receive my punishment. I am on the edge of a metamorphosis, the scary fact that I am self-aware of the tragedy coming my way, and maybe if I do transform into the one thing I hate to become, I will never know what I was like, and I will never know what I was experiencing. I was hit with the sense of reality. I yearn to escape this hellhole soon, and it is now or never. If I ever get to muster up the courage and free myself blindly without being cautious of my surroundings, will I really make it out alive? Well, culturally, it is worth taking a shot. Time is ticking, and the chances are getting slimmer. My paranoia will drain my sanity quicker the longer I keep it up. I should perhaps check whether I am safe from danger. Maybe a peek wouldn't hurt, would it?
==Entities== The majority of entities that inhabit this level casually reside in '''Level 45''''s caves for most of their time. These include [[Entity 3|smilers]], [[Entity 8|hounds]], [[Entity 4|deathmoths]], [[Entity 5|clumps]], and the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. Regardless, multiple level-exclusive entities have been discovered to wander/inhabit the level. These entities are mostly native to the level itself; however, some, if not even a few, are not. |-|Strands= '''Strands''' are a level-exclusive entity that inhabit the outdoors of '''Level 45''', with their appearance identical to a cloudy wisp and a bright blue aura flowing out of them. Previously classified as a non-entity, '''Strands''' were thought to be a mere anomaly upon the discovery of the level; however, following further investigations and analysis, it was confirmed that the anomaly was an entity, not just an anomaly. ---- {{Blurimage|file=F71fd6c67b3737031393c450c8632335.jpg|caption=An image of a '''Strand'''.}} These creatures tend to follow wanderers and are very passive, though they have trouble communicating due to their limited mental capacity; however, the sound these organisms produce is identical to a high-pitched human voice. These creatures will stop following an individual if the person gets bored or walks into a hostile environment, especially caves. '''Strands''' will not indeed pursue advance towards the caves due to the sheer danger and harmful fluctuation and cannot guard their wanderer, they wait for the wanderer to return for the opportunity to tail on their person again in hopes that the individual perhaps didn't pass away, they may also lose their attention and wander off for themselves or fortunately get distracted by another wanderer and instead would follow their new partner. '''Strands''' will warn wanderers of a nearby threat by making a loud yelping noise without the knowledge of physically navigating before acknowledging the presence of danger; they will also guard the outer bounds of the area where the threat is located and may try to block a wanderer from progressing there. This entity's neutral behavior is very peaceful and barely chooses to engage in fighting. A humorous way to get a '''Strands''' to react is to throw an object at them as it passes through them. It isn't confirmed whether this is how '''Strands''' play, or whether they feel threatened to a minor degree, but in retaliation, it will cutely throw back the object their owner originally threw at them. If their wanderer is exposed to danger from a hostile entity, it will block the line of sight to both the wanderer and the predator, and, unfortunately, given that the entity is only a floating gas, it will be an ineffective strategy, since they have no other viable capabilities. When their wanderer encounters another, the '''Strand''' that met with the person first will not have any thoughts about abandoning their owner and will remain by their partner's side, unless their owner gets unfortunately killed or enters a dangerous environment; then it will reconsider following the wanderer they met before and trace their path searching for their new owner. |-|Cerberus= '''Cerberus''' is a mysterious singular entity that roams and watches over the outer boundaries of '''Level 45''', and it's a piece of information that doesn't have many confirmed factors about its existence, as it is hardly ever spotted amongst travelers; a legend with proof of its existence, but with roughly any detail given. ---- '''Cerberus''' appears to be a creature attached to the roots of nearby trees wherever it moves, in the silhouette of a lanky humanoid figure with a crown of its own. The roots of nearby trees, intertwined with the creature, will snap if it eventually reaches certain boundaries; as it gets closer to more trees, their roots will also branch outwards and merge with the entity. {{Blurimage|file=Cerberus.png|caption=A sighting of '''Cerberus'''.}} '''Cerberus''' holds the ability to manipulate fire from the source of their hands, it only uses this ability when it feels threatened or becomes in an aggressive state of mind; the fire becomes a slow projectile and will launch from the force of the owners hand, burning anything in its radius when it comes in indirect contact with, as for direct contact it will obliterate the range with a singed hole. It doesn't have a voice as well. There is vague information circulating around '''Cerberus''' because it's only been a tradition from wanderers who were unfortunate to witness it. However, it is distinct that its behavior is aggressive, and it will do anything to seek its prey, disregarding the minimal resolve it has, as it will get worn out from giving chase due to its miraculously low speed. Drastic measures were taken in an attempt to hunt down the beast, all of which were unsuccessful, with insufficient analysis and conclusions drawn from their battles. The only piece of evidence that found this operation ineffective was when a member of the [[M.E.G.]] discovered a sizzled and severed hand buried underneath a stack of leaves near the coordinates of the set battle. It is heavily inhibited from staying on the premises of '''Cerberus''' as most unlucky people who met the fate of its unity have never been reported to have survived. |-|Glow Larvae= '''Glow Larvae''', otherwise dubbed as "The Lights of the Caves," are siphonophore-like creatures possessing bioluminescent gas and are another level-exclusive entity for [[Level 45]]. There exist multiple instances of the '''Glow Larvae''' appearing in other levels, mainly [[Level 8]] and other levels possessing an appearance of a cave system; however, most lack sufficient evidence/proof. ---- {{Blurimage|file=GlowlarvaeLevel45.png|caption=The '''Glow Larvae''' in their habitat.}} From its self-explanatory name, '''Glow Larvae''' are miniscule blobs that inhabit crystalline caves and live off of absorbing the moist and damp liquid from the environment. Their organs have a bright turquoise glow from the neon gas; this is why they can be seen: these creatures don't have normal bodies and are translucent, like jellyfish. The glow from their bodies can indicate their energy; when they are alert and active, their gases will transition to an excited state, and therefore more molecules will progress more vigorously. Fortunately, '''Glow Larvae''' do not tend to carry parasites or bacterial diseases, but they can pose a risk due to their translucent bodies and the ease with which germs can tunnel through them. '''Glow Larvae''' prefer to dwell on the ceilings of caves more than the ground; the reason is that the floor's water in caves collects more polluted chloride and would morally choose to consume the water seeping through holes from the ceiling. Most of '''Glow Larvae''' have a shorter life span and proceed to lay sticky eggs that glue to the ceiling, and it is why a few of the larvae, which are older, can be found on the ground living their last moments. Due to their miniature size, '''Glow Larvae''' are incapable of posing a serious threat. |-|Hunchbacks= '''Hunchbacks''' are omnivores (preferably carnivores) and level-exclusive entities that inhabit the deep undergrounds of [[Level 45]]. ---- '''Hunchbacks''' take the appearance of a long and lanky humanoid figure with pale and wrinkly skin over their body and having protracted limbs; their backs are curved forward and bulged from their tendency to navigate on both their arms and legs at the same time, hence why they were given the name '''Hunchbacks''' due to their poor posture. Their anatomy is abnormal compared to many humanoid creatures in the [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]], with unique bone blades on their upper thighs providing a layer of protection, and how they walk with their heels elevated off the ground, sharp toes and fingers to match up the incision from an ordinary combat blade. Their arms are long enough to straighten and reach to their ankles. {{Blurimage|file=Hunchbacks.jpg|caption=An illustration of a '''Hunchbacks'''.}} They use their hands to their advantage to slit and wound their prey with potential; inflicted damage from the creature can induce infections and high-speed spreading bacteria. '''Hunchbacks''' have poor physical health, as they rarely bathe to maintain hygiene, and, in addition, their self-explanatory bad posture. Their horrid roars can reach up to a disturbing 110db (decibels), producing detrimental damage to the ears. Despite their dominance due to their monstrous height, their speed is slower, at a rate comparable to that of an average human. Often, their prey outrun them during the chase, making them prone to starvation and dying sooner after their long trek. These creatures are still more numerous, as they inhabit the deep cavities and are dispersed widely and evenly throughout the system of caves. '''Hunchbacks''' are constantly entering and dying within the level because of the horrific fact that these creatures are hatched from concealed craters from [[The Hive]]. An antagonistic place responsible for producing entities always lying and hatching, the source of where all entities come from and traverse to reach levels in the [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]]. ==Colonies & Outposts== Regardless of possessing a habitable environment with enough supply of food and drinkable water, '''Level 45''' remains uninhabited under unknown conditions by humans and is completely devoid of life other than the aforementioned entity(s). Many communities gathered to attempt to form a successful territory, but all the experiments were abandoned due to the bizarre circumstances and the unstable habitat that'''Level 45''' holds. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *Noclipping through a blue tree in [[Level 455]]. *Losing consciousness within [[Level 13]] is an indication of a nearby exit to '''Level 45'''. *Wander into a forest from [[Level 39]], pursuing deeper, which will eventually transition the level's appearance to '''Level 45''' and therefore end up here. *Noclipping through the edges of the gazebo in [[Lurking Demise]] may take one here. *Entering a forest within [[Level 9.6]] will lead to this level. *Noclipping through an oak tree on [[Level 887]] will lead to this level. *Overgrown walls in [[Level 216]] send one here. ===Exits=== *Wandering deeper and deeper into the hazardous caves may lead one to [[The Hive]]. This method is strongly discouraged and shall not be attempted under any circumstances. *Locating and Noclipping through a Cherry Blossom will take you to [[Level 110]]. *Nocliping through a door will take you to [[Level 974]]. *Reaction to intoxication from overdoses of Neon Water and miraculously surviving the disorientating influence will bring one to [[Level 7]]. Do not attempt this, as most often victims will die if not drinking the water carefully. *Noclipping into unstable trees consistently bring wanderers to [[Level 0]]. *Certain unforeseen areas in '''Level 45''''s caves can transition and lead to [[Level 8]]. *Dry areas in this level may lead one to [[Level 46]]. *Discovering a sewer system in the caves of '''Level 45''' will lead to [[Level 49]]. *Broken-down neon signs with the word 'Range' are typically a surefire way to reach [[Level 73]]. *Certain tight caverns lead to [[level 64]]. {{Redirect|Prev=44|Next=46}} ----
